title: Agent Manifest and Markdown Skill Harness Specification
summary: Draft architecture spec for database-backed marketplace agents: reusable package/version definitions composed from system prompt, model/intelligence, goals, Markdown skills, connectors, MCP/tool catalog, budgets, and per-user installed agent instances executed by a generic harness.
next_action: Monitor runtime behavior for installed manifest-backed agents and use follow-up specs for external publishing, ratings, or third-party marketplace submission flows.

verification.notes: Implemented phases 0-6. Targeted tests passed for marketplace manifests, Markdown skills/tool catalog, agent package lifecycle, manifest source-behavior delegation, default operator installation, and source-backed morning briefing. Full mix precommit passed with 1070 tests, 0 failures.

resume_context.last_completed_step: Implemented phases 0-6 for manifest-backed marketplace agents, moved active product intelligence into Markdown skill packs, and assembled the default Chief of Staff package into runtime bootstrap for the primary operator.
pending_steps: Use follow-up specs for external marketplace publishing, package ratings, and third-party submission review if needed
resume_context.notes: Marketplace agents are reusable database-driven packages with package versions, changelogs, install/pause/remove/upgrade operations, explicit model/intelligence policy, required loadable Markdown skills for manifest-backed agents, connector/MCP/tool metadata, run observability, default primary-operator installation, manifest source-behavior delegation for existing acquisition/delivery loops, and no heuristic fallback for source-backed briefing generation.

history.note: Initial draft created for manifest-driven Markdown skill harness architecture.
Expanded architecture to treat agents as database-backed marketplace packages with immutable package versions and per-user installed agent instances.

Completed phases 0-6: package/install split, manifest envelope, Markdown skill loader, generic agent loop, tool/MCP catalog, database-driven marketplace operations, and compatibility-shim migration away from behavior-specific product intelligence.
Manifest-backed package sync and direct package-version changesets now require at least one Markdown skill path, and sync validates that the referenced skills load.

Runtime bootstrap now syncs built-in packages, installs the default Chief of Staff package for the primary operator, resumes it as a manifest-backed agent, delegates scheduled cycles through source-behavior shims, and has briefing cron coverage for manifest-installed Chief of Staff agents.
